description: GA model collapse finding on CIFAR-100 — new empirical contribution
finding: Gradient Ascent with lr=0.010 (calibrated for CIFAR-10) causes model collapse on CIFAR-100 (100-class task). Confidence scores converge to uniform distribution (~1/100 = 0.01), producing undefined MIA AUC. The VeriForgot oracle correctly returns no compliance certificate in this case.
practical_guideline: GA learning rate must scale inversely with task complexity. Recommended: lr=0.010 for <=10 classes, lr=0.005 for 100 classes.
scrub_robustness: SCRUB shows zero failures across all dataset/LR configurations, making it the recommended default for production VeriForgot deployments.
paper_section: Section 5.3 — Hyperparameter Sensitivity and Method Robustness
